Mood · Al Satwa · Jul 2026
Mood, Al Satwa · Owner Proposal — Hold & Reposition — Confidential

Retain & reposition to realize Mood’s full potential

Flow proposes to reposition the 152 units from short-stay accommodation into Flow-operated long-stay residences. This is expected to increase your annual income from approximately AED 6.2 million to AED 10.2 million, including a proposed minimum rent of AED 7.5 million. This results in AED 105 million of incremental asset value*.

The plan
You hold the asset — Flow repositions short-stay → long-stay and operates
Your outlay
AED 11.6M — recovered in Year 1
The outcome
Higher NOI → higher valuation → stronger exit
*Based on a cap rate of 7.0%, with a sale in year 3 of operation
01 · Our Proposition

Today’s situation

Strong assets. An inefficient operating model.

Mood 22 and Mood 45 are well-located, characterful buildings with strong amenities and an established revenue base. The quality of the real estate is not being reflected in the annual returns and therefore the property valuation.

01
Strong underlying real estate
The buildings already provide the character, amenities and unit mix required for a compelling residential proposition. The opportunity is to enhance the product, not fundamentally reinvent it.
02
A respectable, but unexceptional, topline
The short-stay model generates approximately AED 15.3 million of annualised revenue. This demonstrates genuine demand, but the current operating model does not maximise the revenue potential of the 152 units.
03
A disproportionately high cost base
Approximately AED 9.1 million, or 60% of revenue, is absorbed by operating costs. Nightly turnover, housekeeping, OTA commissions, utilities and hotel-style staffing leave approximately AED 6.2 million for you.

The Flow opportunity

Increase the topline. Compress the cost base. Widen your income margin.

Flow’s long-stay model is designed to increase annual revenue from approximately AED 15.3 million to AED 18.7 million, while reducing property operating costs from approximately 60% to 20% of revenue. This allows a materially greater proportion of the income generated by the assets to flow through to the owner.

REVENUE
AED 15.3M → AED 18.7M
OPERATING-COST RATIO
60% → 20%
OWNER INCOME
AED 6.2M → AED 10.2M

Prove the income. Strengthen the exit.

The assets are currently offered at approximately AED 170 million, but their existing income profile is likely to support a materially lower buyer valuation. Flow’s repositioning is designed to close this gap by replacing a high-cost short-stay model with a higher, recurring and contract-backed long-stay income stream.

Once stabilised, the assets will no longer be sold on potential and land value appreciation. They can be valued on a clean, operational asset basis, supporting a stronger refinancing or exit outcome.

Today
Asking priceAED 170M
Flow acquisition underwriting based on current performanceAED 120M
After Flow stabilisation
Capitalisable annual income — Year 3~AED 13.5M
Illustrative value at a 7.0% capitalisation rate — Year-3 exit~AED 193M
The value gap, visualised

03 · The Market Evidence

Driving your revenue from AED 15.3M to AED 18.7M

Flow’s rents are underwritten against 1,105 rental transactions in Al Satwa from the past 12 months. Five key comparable projects have been identified: Eden House, Hyde Walk, Coral by Vision, Oasis 1 and Jasmine House.

Eden House, a luxury build-to-rent development, has set a new benchmark for the area, with Hyde Walk and Coral performing at strong rates also, albeit at a more upscale product offer.

04 · Deal Mechanics

How the deal works

You keep the asset and fund the works — Flow takes no ownership, repositions, brands and operates, and is paid from performance. Your CapEx is repaid first, in Year 1.

01
You fund the CapEx, our design team gets to work
Every unit refreshed and the amenities brought to the Flow standard — repaid to you in full in Year 1.
AED 11.6M
02
We lease up the building during Dubai’s peak rental seasons
Q4 is always the peak of the lettings market in Dubai. The ideal time to begin a lease-up.
~5 months
03
The waterfall
Income splits in strict order — illustrated against a stabilised year.
1
Minimum rent — paid to you first, guaranteed
From Year 2 (12-month rent-free during reposition); steps up 5% in Year 6, then every 5 years.
More than the buildings’ entire income today (~AED 6.2M)
AED 7.5M
2
CapEx repayment — Year 1
Your AED 11.6M works cost is returned in full, senior to every Flow fee.
AED 11.6M
3
Management fee
Flow’s base fee for operating, leasing and brand.
10% of revenue
4
Performance fee
10% of revenue, earned as the buildings perform.
10% of revenue
5
Profit share
Everything remaining: three quarters yours, one Flow’s.
75 / 25
A stabilised year · Year 2
Total revenue~AED 18.7M
Operating expenses — 20%−AED 3.7M
Minimum rent → youAED 7.5M
Management fee → Flow — 10%−AED 1.9M
Performance fee → Flow — 10%−AED 1.9M
Replacement reserve — 0.7% of revenue−AED 0.1M
Residual — split 75 / 25AED 3.6M
Total to you — min rent + AED 2.7M share~AED 10.2M
04
The outcome
Increased annual income — and the asset valuation that follows it.
